title: buildah manifest push fails with "manifest blob unknown: blob unknown to registry" when not add --all
body:
**Description**
I create a new manifest and wanna push it to registry first time, push fails if I haven't added --all. I'm not sure whether the way I use buildah manifest is correct, why --all is necessary?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.buildah manifest create newone
2.buildah manifest add --tls-verify=false newone docker://localhost:5000/busybox_x86
3.buildah manifest push --tls-verify=false -f v2s2 newone docker://localhost:5000/newone

**Describe the results you received:**
Error messages:
Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(Error uploading manifest latest to localhost:5000/newone: manifest blob unknown: blob unknown to registry)


**Version**
➜  buildah git:(master) buildah version                                                                                 
Version:         1.18.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.13.3
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.2-0.20190904153231-83439463f784
image Version:   5.7.0
Git Commit:      5c27e00
Built:           Wed Nov  4 14:58:05 2020
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

comments.body: @Vanient the --all is required in the manifest push here, without `--all` it just pushes the manifest itself without the images in it. The registry can reject the manifest list because the registry doesn't have it for that repository.  Use `--all` will go through each item from the manifest list and check if the registry already has it, and try to only upload it if the registry doesn't already have it.

> @Vanient the --all is required in the manifest push here, without `--all` it just pushes the manifest itself without the images in it. The registry can reject the manifest list because the registry doesn't have it for that repository. Use `--all` will go through each item from the manifest list and check if the registry already has it, and try to only upload it if the registry doesn't already have it.

Thanks for reply, but still have question for that. What the "The registry can reject the manifest list because the registry doesn't have it for that repository" means? Images in the list already exists at the registry, why should I push them again not just push the list? Or Maybe you could give me an example, in what kind of case I could not add --all to push.

@Vanient From my understanding, the -all will try to push the images in the manifest list fist(check if the image is already there and attempt to upload it), and then the list itself. WIthout the --all, only the manifest list will be pushed, the registry may reject the list since it references a manifest that the registry doesn't have. There is some [explanation](https://lists.podman.io/archives/list/podman%40lists.podman.io/message/EIYJSUW4FM4QXSESJK4GAUA74GOHLCUF/) also.
